Article: The Renewal Project
[]: The Renewal Project             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Wed Feb 27, 2008 1:15 pm At the risk of sounding like a NOOB, which I have no intention of being, I want to ask for a big favour. Can anyone give me a list of everything, and I mean EVERYTHING that was part of the Renewal of Evangelion project. To cut a long story short, I am an aspiring Evangelion collector, and to me, all the content of Renewal is essential. I've tried to find an exact list, but there's quite a bit of stuff, so I haven't had any luck. I would be most grateful.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Feb 27, 2008 4:02 pm I'm not at home right now, but if it helps any when I ordered those two Eva games (Ayanami with Asuka Raising Project and Evangelion2), they came with a Renewal Products News pamphlet. One is Product News:01, the other is 02. While it's not every single thing mentioned, at least it's a start. ;) (Would uploading a photo of those or scanning those in be okay?)

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Wed Feb 27, 2008 5:12 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: (Would uploading a photo of those or scanning those in be okay?)

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Feb 27, 2008 6:29 pm It seems like they were, yes. (Same with Girlfriend of Steel 2, since there's an ad for the game in there somewhere, too.)

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Feb 28, 2008 8:58 pm I should be able to scan in more useful stuff next week. First one is from Die Sterne Artbook, second one is from the PS2 Eva 2 game. (My Ayanami with Asuka game has the version of Asuka & Rei hugging on the cover, not the original(??) cover with them and their Evas. Maybe that's why it doesn't have one of these??)

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Feb 28, 2008 11:52 pm How does the "Renewal of Eva" project differ from the

Article: The Renewal Project
 that's why it doesn't have one of these??)

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Feb 28, 2008 11:52 pm How does the "Renewal of Eva" project differ from the "Renewal of Evangelion" DVD sets (known in the USA as "Platinum")

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Feb 29, 2008 6:31 pm The DVD sets are PART of the Renewal project.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Mar 01, 2008 12:45 pm ok what's the rest of the Renewal project besides the DVDs?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Mar 03, 2008 6:17 pm I'm getting there, V. ;) The rest of the scans will be uploaded later today.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Mar 04, 2008 1:38 pm ....Or today instead of yesterday. (And I'm not certain why some of the scans are sized differently, but better than nothing I guess.) Anyway, from Products News booklet 01: And Products News 02: EDIT: So I just tried cropping a pic and the quality become rather shitty for lack of a better word. Guess I'll leave these as is then. (At least for the time being.)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Mar 04, 2008 1:56 pm Your software won't let you crop? Just curious.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Mar 04, 2008 2:31 pm D'oh! Thank goodness for online image editors. *fixes it* EDIT: See above edit.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Mar 04, 2008 5:34 pm Oh, thing is, when you're dealing with image files, the rule is to do all editing BEFORE the file goes into a compressed format, namely JPEG, in the first place. JPEGs are full of ugly compression artifacts -- extent and visibility depends on degree of compression -- and they're essentially being compressed again every time they're saved, providing an additive degenerative effect. Your files already have, at their default magnification, huge artifacts that are visible to the naked eye, which is, in grunty-s

Article: The Renewal Project
 -- and they're essentially being compressed again every time they're saved, providing an additive degenerative effect. Your files already have, at their default magnification, huge artifacts that are visible to the naked eye, which is, in grunty-speak, a "VERY BAD THING!". Unless you still have original, uncompressed PNGs (or whatever) of your scans lying around, they're only going to look worse from here on out.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Tue Mar 04, 2008 5:52 pm So, from what I can tell from those, Renewal includes: The platinum discs. Fly me to the moon + Refrain of Evangelion Cd's Die Sterne Girlfriend of Steel 2 Ayanami/Asuka Raising Project Angelic Days Evangelions 2 And a bugger-load of dolls. Anything I missed?

[]:             Quote Post by Master Hand » Fri Apr 11, 2008 4:13 pm Wow.That's a lot of Eva stuff.I know it's popular but never knew it was THIS popular.Too bad I cant read Japanese to know what those images say.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sat Apr 12, 2008 4:58 pm Sodding parent control (still trying to crack it) doesn't let me see it. Can anybody put it into text format (original Japanese please)?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon May 12, 2008 1:42 pm I cropped those other pics so the scanner shouldn't be as noticeable as it was before. I'm in no hurry to rescan it since I thought the point of this was just let it be to known what merchandise was available; not so much the booklets themselves. (However, if somebody would want me to rescan em in at some point for better quality (Reichu??), just let me know.) From my Neon Genesis Evangelion Decade Memorial Box (inside the case of the Another Cases game):

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue May 13, 2008 7:44 pm Bump for anybody interested.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Wed May 14, 2008 1:01 am Thanks very much for doing this. I only wish I could BLOODY read the Japanese!!!

Article: The Renewal Project
[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Wed May 14, 2008 1:01 am Thanks very much for doing this. I only wish I could BLOODY read the Japanese!!!

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed May 14, 2008 12:43 pm Glad to be of service. Anyway from the few things I can read, it mostly gives brief summaries/points of the games and such.
